###### This folder generated by claude for the question: "Write Code for a REST API"

Claude
Write Code for a REST API


Sure—I can give you a solid starting point. Below is a minimal, secure REST API using Python with FastAPI, SQLAlchemy (SQLite for simplicity), and JWT-based authentication. It includes:

User registration and login (passwords hashed with bcrypt)
Protected CRUD endpoints for an example resource (Item)
OpenAPI/Swagger docs automatically provided by FastAPI
Basic input validation with Pydantic
Basic error handling
Environment-configurable settings
If you prefer Node.js/Express or Go, I can provide those as well.

### Directory structure (example)
app/
    main.py
    models.py
    schemas.py
    database.py
    uth.py
endpoints/
    items.py
    users.py
core/
    config.py
require_https.py (optional)
requirements.txt
alembic/ (optional for migrations)
.env (for secrets)



### Running the API
Install: pip install -r requirements.txt
Run: uvicorn app.main:app --reload
Docs: http://127.0.0.1:8000/docs (Swagger) or /redoc
Notes and improvements:

Security: Use HTTPS in production, store SECRET_KEY in environment (e.g., via .env), implement proper password rules, and consider rate limiting.
Persistence: Swap SQLite for PostgreSQL/MySQL in production; adjust DATABASE_URL accordingly.
Token security: Consider token revocation strategy, longer-term refresh tokens, and audience/issuer claims.
Validation: Add more robust validation (length checks, email domain validation).
Testing: Add unit and integration tests with pytest and httpx.
CI/CD: Add linting, type checking, and tests in your pipeline.
Documentation: FastAPI auto-generates docs; you can add summary and tags in routers for clarity.
If you share your preferred language, framework, and resource model (e.g., User, Product, Order), I’ll tailor a complete, ready-to-run example to your stack.

### how to use require_https.py
How to use it

..Import and add the middleware to your FastAPI app in main.py (before or after other middlewares as needed).
You can enable/disable via an environment variable or the constructor.
from fastapi import FastAPI
from .database import Base, engine
from .endpoints import users, items
from .require_https import RequireHTTPSMiddleware

# create tables
Base.metadata.create_all(bind=engine)

app = FastAPI(title="Secure REST API")

# Enforce HTTPS (adjust DEBUG as needed)
app.add_middleware(RequireHTTPSMiddleware, debug=False)

# Include routers
app.include_router(users.router, prefix="/auth", tags=["auth"])
app.include_router(items.router, prefix="/api", tags=["items"])
